data.title: ASML Holding NV expected to post earnings of €5.68 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• ASML Holding NV , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 16 for the period ending March 31 2025

  • The Veldhoven Noord-brabant-based company is expected to report a 45.4% increase in revenue to €7.691 billion from €5.29 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 7 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for ASML Holding NV is for earnings of €5.68 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 13 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.4% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for ASML Holding NV is $892.50​, above​ its last closing price of $668.81. ​​​

data.title: BNP Paribas Expects Closing of Acquisition of AXA Investment Managers in Early July
NZX 50 Closes Higher on US Tariff Relief
data.description:
New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index closed 0.7% higher to 12,108 on Monday, reversing losses from the previous session, following tariff exemptions on select tech products that eased concerns over the US trade policy.

However, caution lingered as President Trump and Commerce Secretary Lutnick signaled the move may be temporary, with Trump set to announce semiconductor tariffs next week, possibly with flexibility for some firms. Domestically, fresh survey data showed New Zealand’s services sector contracted for a second consecutive month, while card spending declined—adding to signs of weakening economic conditions highlighted by the RBNZ following last week’s rate cut to 3.5%.

Most sectors were in the green, with finance and miscellaneous shares leading the advance.

Notable gains included Westpac Banking (+2.9%), ANZ Group (+2.3%), and Infratil (+5.4%).

data.title: Australia's Koonenberry Gold surges on gold find at NSW project
data.description:
** Shares of Koonenberry Gold up 15.1% at A$0.061, after rising as much as 30.2% earlier in the day

** Mineral explorer identifies significant gold mineralization at the Enmore Gold Project in New South Wales

** Stock on track for its third consecutive session of gains

** About 41.1 mln shares traded in stock's busiest session since April 2

** Stock up ~361% YTD, including session's gains

data.title: Ircon International Secures Order Worth INR1.28 Billion
data.description:
Ircon International has been awarded an order worth 1.28 billion Indian rupees from the S&T (Construction), North Western Railway, Indian Railways, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The contract is for the design, manufacture, supply, installation, testing, and commissioning of a microprocessor-based EI (enable interrupt) system at 20 stations of the Ajmer division along with automatic block signaling and associated indoor and outdoor works of signaling and telecom systems.

The project is expected to be completed in 24 months.

data.title: LondonMetric Property Makes Approach to Buy Urban Logistics
data.description:
By Ian Walker

LondonMetric Property said late Friday that it made a cash and share proposal to buy Urban Logistics REIT.

The U.K. real-estate investment trust said that it made the approach on April 2 and was awaiting a reply from Urban Logistics.

Separately Urban Logistics said it had received the approach from LondonMetric and was reviewing it with its advisors.

No financial details of any deal has been disclosed by either company and both have cautioned that there is no certainty any deal will be made.

LondonMetric had a market capitalization of 3.74 billion pounds ($4.89 billion) and Urban Logistics was worth 595.3 million pounds based on their closing share prices of 182.1 pence and 128 pence respectively on Friday.

LondonMetric has until May 9 to either make an offer to buy Urban Logistics or walk away under U.K. Takeover Panel rules.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: Huagong Tech's Q1 Profit to Jump Up to 52%
data.description:
Huagong Tech expects its net profit attributable to shareholders in the first quarter to jump between 35% and 52% to a range of 390 million yuan to 440 million yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Saturday.

Earnings per share will be between 0.39 yuan and 0.44 yuan.

The year-ago profit was 289.9 million yuan or 0.29 yuan per share, the Chinese laser technology developer said.

The company's shares jumped less than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Atlas Pearls Reports Total Revenue of AU$7.5 Million from Pearl Auction in Japan
data.description:
Atlas Pearls reported that total revenue from an auction of pearls held by the firm in Kobe, Japan on April 8 and April 9, amounted to AU$7.5 million, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The firm presented 70,592 pearls and sold 58,345 pearls at an average price of AU$129 per piece, which was an increase from a price of AU$73 per piece in the previous auction in December 2024, the filing said.

Stock withheld from the April auction, amounting to around 12,000 pearls, is expected to realize better value via auction through other sales channels.

The next major auction will be held in Kobe in June, per the filing.

data.title: BNP Paribas Sees Closing Of AXA Investment Managers Acquisition In Early July
data.description:
BNP Paribas SA :

  • ACQUISITION BY BNP PARIBAS CARDIF OF AXA INVESTMENT MANAGERS – UPDATE

  • CLOSING IS EXPECTED IN EARLY JULY 2025

  • DIVIDEND AND SHAREHOLDER RETURN POLICY UNCHANGED

  • IMPACT ON BNP PARIBAS CET1 RATIO EXPECTED AT -35 BPS

  • NEITHER GROUP’S OVERALL PROFITABILITY OBJECTIVES, GROWTH TRAJECTORY, NOR ITS EQUITY AND CET1 TRAJECTORY WOULD BE MODIFIED

  • EXPECTED RETURN ON INVESTED CAPITAL OF TRANSACTION WOULD BE ABOVE 14% IN 3RD YEAR AND MORE THAN 20% IN 4TH YEAR

  • LAUNCH OF SHARE BUYBACK PROGRAMME, ANNOUNCED IN FEBRUARY 2025, TO WHICH ECB HAS ALREADY GIVEN ITS APPROVAL, IS MAINTAINED

data.title: Atua AI Expands XRP Cryptocurrency Stack to Support Advanced Financial Automation
data.description:
Enhanced XRP Integration Enables Faster, Smarter Transactions and AI-Driven Workflows Across Web3

Dubai, United Arab Emirates--(Newsfile Corp. - April 14, 2025) - Atua AI (TUA), the decentralized AI-powered productivity platform, has expanded its XRP cryptocurrency stack to enable more advanced and efficient financial automation across multichain ecosystems. This enhancement strengthens the platform's ability to deliver real-time, AI-supported solutions for payments, yield optimization, risk analysis, and asset management.

Advanced financial automation meets real-time performance and multichain speed

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8833/248374_4056787f2492c882_001full.jpg

With deeper XRP integration, Atua AI users can now automate complex financial operations with improved speed, lower transaction costs, and higher scalability. From executing on-chain payment flows to triggering intelligent financial actions through AI-powered tools like Chat and Writer, the platform brings precision and real-time responsiveness to DeFi and enterprise finance.

Atua AI's upgraded XRP stack also supports seamless data exchange and smart contract interaction, enabling businesses to build automated systems for treasury operations, compliance monitoring, and user activity analysis. The TUA token acts as the utility gateway, connecting users to XRP-backed tools that drive financial performance and AI-led decision-making.

This expansion reflects Atua AI's commitment to building secure, interoperable, and high-speed solutions at the intersection of AI and blockchain. By leveraging XRP's proven infrastructure, the platform delivers the agility and efficiency needed to scale financial automation across the decentralized Web3 economy.

About Atua AI

Atua AI offers AI-powered productivity and creativity tools in the Web3 space. Its features include Chat, Writer, Imagine, Voiceover, and Classifier—all designed to empower users with intelligent, decentralized solutions for content creation, coding, analysis, and more.

data.title: IHH Healthcare Issues 717,000 Shares Upon Exercise of Warrants Under Incentive Scheme
data.description:
IHH Healthcare issued 717,000 shares pursuant to the exercise of options under the company's Enterprise Option Schemes, according to a Monday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The dual-listed company issued 481,000 shares at 5.92 Malaysian ringgit each and 236,000 shares at 5.87 ringgit per share.

The issued shares will be listed April 15.

data.description:
SHELF DRILLING ANNUAL REPORT 2024

Dubai, April 14, 2025: Shelf Drilling, Ltd. ("Shelf Drilling" and, togetherwithits subsidiaries, the "Company", OSE: SHLF) announces publication of its 2024Annual Report.

Contact detailsFor questions or comments, please contactinvestor.relations@shelfdrilling.com.

About Shelf DrillingShelf Drilling is a leading international shallow water offshore drillingcontractor with rig operations across Middle East, Southeast Asia, India, WestAfrica, Mediterranean and North Sea. Shelf Drilling was founded in 2012 andhasestablished itself as a leader within its industry through its fit-for-purposestrategy and close working relationship with industry leading clients. Thecompany is incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands with corporateheadquarters in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. The company is listed on the OsloStock Exchange under the ticker "SHLF".

Additional information about Shelf Drilling can be found atwww.shelfdrilling.com.

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to section5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

data.title: Tariff relief on tech goods drives rally in China, Hong Kong shares
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks rose on Monday as tech shares climbed after the White House exempted smartphones and computers from "reciprocal" U.S. tariffs, while the chip sector was weighed down by national security concerns.

China's blue-chip CSI300 Index climbed 0.5%, while the Shanghai Composite Index gained 0.9% by the midday trading break, after losing 2.9% and 3.1% last week, respectively.

Hong Kong benchmark's Hang Seng climbed 2.4% after an 8.5% loss last week. The Hang Seng Tech Index added 2.7%.

Trump's administration granted exclusions from steep tariffs on smartphones, computers and other electronics imported largely from China, while indicating these goods will come under separate tariffs, along with semiconductors, that may be imposed within a month.

China's Apple suppliers jumped on the temporary relief. The CSI Electronics Index (.CSI930652) jumped 0.9% by midday. Apple's main assembly partner Foxconn Industrial Internet rallied 1.1%, and acoustic components manufacturer Goertek jumped 2.5%.

Keeping a lid on gains, the CSI Semiconductor Index (.CSI931865) declined as much as 0.9% after Trump said that chips from China will face a national security probe over the next week.

The news of the exemption "fuels expectation of potential improvement in trade relations, and even the prospect of trade negotiations," said Li Shuding, an investor adviser of Galaxy Securities.

He added that March loan data was better than expected, and "we expect intensive roll-out of policies" to support the economy.

Before Monday's rebound, the CSI 300 Index and Hang Seng Index had lost 3.5% and 9.9%, respectively, since "Liberation Day" on April 2. Trump threatened to implement sweeping tariffs against trading partners with China being the major target, raising concerns about slowing global economic growth and disruptions to trade.

Goldman Sachs has revised its 12-month targets for MSCI China and the CSI300 Index, lowering them to 75 and 4,300, respectively, down from previous estimates of 81 and 4,500, respectively.

"U.S.-China trade tensions have soared to unprecedented levels, prompting concerns about global recession, and decoupling risks between the two largest economies globally in other strategic cohorts, notably capital markets, technology, and geopolitics," China strategist Kinger Lau said in a note on Monday.

Elsewhere, China's exports in March rose 12.4% from a year earlier, handily beating 4.4% growth expected in a Reuters poll of economists after factories rushed out shipments before the latest U.S. tariffs took effect.

data.title: North American Miners Face Continued Jolts Without U.S.-China Trade DealMarket Talk
data.description: Shares of North American base metals producers are likely to remain volatile until there is a trade deal between the U.S. and China, Jefferies analyst Christopher LaFemina says in a note. Stocks in those companies rose 13% on average last Wednesday, tumbled Thursday, and then rallied again Friday, he says. "Even when this gets resolved, the over-reliance of mining on China justifies low valuations," says LaFemina, adding that higher commodity prices and earnings would be essential for a strong recovery. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)
